description: Extends the MATH-11 beta-content decomposition from A2 to A3. At two loops, A2 decomposes into beta^0 (rational + zeta) and beta^2 (pi^2 terms) with 90.4% cancellation. At three loops, A3 adds beta^4 content (pi^4 term) and a mixed beta^2*zeta term. Tracking how the cancellation evolves from 2 to 3 loops tests whether the spherical/toroidal tension builds toward the 4-loop breaking point where genus-1 Laporta integrals appear.
thesis: The cancellation between beta-powered (angular/geometric) and beta-free (topological/number-theoretic) terms grows from A2 to A3, building tension that breaks at A4 where genuinely new (toroidal/elliptic) constants enter.
formula: A_n = sum of terms classified as beta^0 (rational, zeta) + beta^2 (pi^2 content) + beta^4 (pi^4 content) + mixed (pi^2 * zeta)
